ncs_standard_definition.description_short: An Agricultural Supply Chain Analyst analyzes and optimizes the flow of goods, services, and information from the farm to the consumer, ensuring efficient and sustainable agricultural supply chains.
ncs_standard_definition.description_detailed: The role involves analyzing data on crop yields, weather patterns, market trends, and transportation networks to identify areas of improvement and develop strategies to reduce costs, increase efficiency, and enhance customer satisfaction.

first_stepper.description: High school students interested in pursuing a career in agricultural supply chain management can start by taking courses in mathematics, statistics, and computer science.

choice_maker.description: Individuals with a generic degree can bridge their skills by taking certifications in supply chain management, data analysis, and agricultural economics.

future_builder.description: Agricultural supply chain analysts with 2-5 years of experience can advance their careers by developing expertise in data science, machine learning, and IoT technologies.
future_builder.prerequisite_experience: 2 years as Junior Supply Chain Analyst

problem_solver_uturn.description: Individuals from unrelated fields can transition into agricultural supply chain analysis by developing skills in data analysis, statistical modeling, and supply chain management.

roadmap.title: Foundations in Supply Chain Management
resources_keywords: Council of Supply Chain Management Professionals (CSCMP)
